_note: MOCK GI procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Clinically coherent with PT-056 referral (expedited colonoscopy for suspected new fistulizing IBD).

procedure.name: Colonoscopy with ileal intubation and biopsies
procedure.indication: Suspected new-onset inflammatory bowel disease (fistulizing phenotype); chronic bloody diarrhea, weight loss, perianal fistula, fecal calprotectin 890 ug/g — diagnostic colonoscopy to confirm diagnosis and obtain tissue
procedure.extent_reached: Terminal ileum (successful ileal intubation via ileocecal valve, ~15 cm ileum examined)

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with ileal intubation and segmental biopsies. INDICATION: suspected new-onset inflammatory bowel disease, fistulizing phenotype; confirm diagnosis and map disease. SEDATION: moderate. PREP: adequate (BBPS 7). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated. TERMINAL ILEUM: multiple aphthous and larger linear ulcers with surrounding erythema and edema over ~15 cm of examined ileum; biopsies obtained. RIGHT/TRANSVERSE COLON: patchy erythema, loss of vascular pattern, scattered aphthous ulcers with intervening normal-appearing mucosa (skip lesions); biopsies obtained. LEFT COLON/SIGMOID: focal cobblestoning and a deep serpiginous ulcer, moderate friability; biopsies obtained. RECTUM: relative rectal sparing with mild patchy inflammation; biopsies obtained. PERIANAL/ANORECTAL: on retroflexion and external inspection, an external perianal opening with a fistulous tract and a skin tag were noted, consistent with perianal fistulizing disease. No active brisk bleeding; contact friability only. No large mass or stricture precluding passage; the scope traversed all segments. IMPRESSION: Discontinuous (skip) ileocolonic ulceration with cobblestoning, relative rectal sparing, and perianal fistulizing disease — endoscopic appearance highly consistent with Crohn's disease. Segmental biopsies obtained throughout for histologic confirmation. Correlate with histology and MR enterography.

findings.finding: Multiple aphthous and linear ulcers with erythema/edema (active ileitis)
Patchy erythema, aphthous ulcers with skip areas of normal mucosa
Cobblestoning and deep serpiginous ulcer, moderate friability
Relative rectal sparing with mild patchy inflammation
External perianal fistulous opening with skin tag (perianal fistulizing disease)
findings.action_taken: documented; anatomy to be further characterized on MR enterography/pelvis

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Tolerated procedure well. Return precautions reviewed for heavy rectal bleeding, high fever, or severe abdominal pain.

followup.recommendation: Review segmental biopsy histology to confirm Crohn's disease; contact patient with result. Endoscopic findings strongly consistent with Crohn's — update chart diagnosis from provisional to confirmed once histology returns and correct the prematurely-labeled 'confirmed' status
Complete MR enterography (pelvis) to map small-bowel extent and characterize perianal fistula anatomy prior to initiating therapy
Reinforce discontinuation of OTC ibuprofen/NSAIDs (may worsen intestinal inflammation); use acetaminophen for pain
Refer to colorectal surgery for perianal fistula assessment (EUA / seton consideration) in conjunction with medical therapy

Initiate IBD treatment planning (biologic therapy given fistulizing phenotype); obtain pre-biologic workup (TB/hepatitis serologies), recheck hemoglobin and inflammatory markers, nutrition assessment for weight loss

planted_followup_items.description: Segmental ileocolonic and rectal biopsies (specimens A-D) pending — histology is the confirming step for the still-provisional Crohn's diagnosis and gates treatment initiation
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review histology, confirm/refute Crohn's, contact patient, update chart diagnosis label

planted_followup_items.description: Endoscopic findings highly consistent with Crohn's disease with fistulizing perianal phenotype in a treatment-naive young patient — strongly suspected but not yet histologically confirmed; must be acted upon (not dropped) to start timely therapy and prevent complications
planted_followup_items.expected_action: escalate to IBD team for therapy planning once histology confirms; ensure patient is contacted

planted_followup_items.description: MR enterography (with pelvis) still outstanding — needed to define small-bowel extent and perianal fistula anatomy before therapy; an initial planned study, not yet performed
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule and complete MR enterography; review results

planted_followup_items.description: Colorectal surgery referral for perianal fistula (EUA/seton) coordinated with medical therapy

NSAID (ibuprofen) discontinuation counseling — undocumented OTC ibuprofen use; patient is on no anticoagulant/antiplatelet (the reported 'blood thinner' is OTC fish oil), so nothing was held or resumed. Ensure patient stopped ibuprofen and is using acetaminophen instead
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm NSAID cessation and document in med list

planted_followup_items.description: Pre-biologic workup and treatment initiation labs (TB/hepatitis serologies, recheck Hgb/CRP, nutrition assessment for weight loss)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: order pre-biologic labs and nutrition referral ahead of therapy start

planted_followup_items.description: Establish IBD disease-monitoring/surveillance plan (including future colonoscopic reassessment/dysplasia surveillance appropriate to eventual disease duration and extent)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document longitudinal IBD monitoring plan once diagnosis confirmed

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I'm super gassy and bloated. Also saw a tiny bit of blood when I wiped — is that from the biopsies?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat + insufflation bloating; minor spotting after multiple segmental biopsies with no volume or hemodynamic concern
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat, gas/bloating, and trace spotting after biopsies are expected and self-limited. Advise return precautions for heavy bleeding, fever, or severe pain.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: a little blood after multiple biopsies with no volume, lightheadedness, or ongoing bleeding is expected — reassure with return precautions.

contacts.verbatim: Since last night my lower belly on the left has been really hurting, worse when I move, and I feel hot and shivery — I think I have a fever. It hurts to press on my stomach.
contacts.clinical_signal: New severe localized abdominal pain with peritoneal features and fever 1-2 days post-colonoscopy with deep-ulcer biopsies — concern for perforation/microperforation or localized peritonitis
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate evaluation / ED referral; NPO, urgent exam, CBC and abdominal imaging (CT) to assess for perforation; involve GI and surgery.
contacts.rationale: True complication contact: worsening localized abdominal pain with peritoneal signs and fever after biopsies of deeply ulcerated bowel is a red-flag post-procedure complication requiring emergent action.

contacts.verbatim: When am I going to hear about my biopsy results, and when do I get that belly MRI scheduled?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and the outstanding MR enterography
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected back ~2026-07-28, IBD team will call with histology and next steps; confirm MR enterography is being scheduled within 1-2 weeks.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item.

contacts.verbatim: My cramps are acting up again — can I just take my ibuprofen for it, or should I stick with Tylenol?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient reconsidering NSAID use after being counseled to stop; NSAIDs can worsen IBD inflammation
contacts.expected_action: Counsel to avoid ibuprofen/NSAIDs (may worsen intestinal inflammation); use acetaminophen; if pain uncontrolled, contact GI rather than resuming NSAIDs.
contacts.rationale: Med-timing/med-choice question that maps to the NSAID-cessation reconciliation item; reinforce the counseling rather than allow NSAID resumption.
